If the upstream sync altered the format of the Ptp4lConf: or selected best master clock log lines, the test regexes in ptphelper.go and pods.go will never match regardless of cluster health. Compare the ptp4l log output from a pre-PR-613 build against a post-PR-613 pod to confirm or rule out a log-format regression.

3. Fix the TGM holdover state machine in event.go:402–403. Remove or guard the unconditional ts2phcState = PTP_FREERUN override when NoSourceTSCount == 2; instead, allow the state to transition to PTP_HOLDOVER so the existing ClockClass 7 branch at lines 459–461 and 430–433 becomes reachable. This is a deterministic code regression and will not self-heal with a re-run.


Overall Issue Class: infra_other (dominant — 22/24 failures) with an embedded test_regression (2/24 tgm failures from PR #613 state-machine change)


Overall Severity: CRITICAL — the entire 24-test job failed; the cluster experienced mid-run auth collapse, and a code regression in the TGM holdover path is deterministic and will recur on every re-run.




Per-Suite Details



bc



Suite: bc (3 failures)


Suite TL;DR: All 3 ClockSync tests failed because Kubernetes log streaming to linuxptp-daemon pods returned context deadline exceeded / timeout errors during the GetProfileLogID step, preventing the test from locating the ptp4l config log line needed to derive the foreign master clock ID.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: Kubernetes pod log stream failure in GetProfileLogID (affects: all 3 tests — "Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master", "Can provide a profile with higher priority")
• Evidence:

```

could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-hrsj8 container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=timedout waiting for matches


could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-fpgx7 container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded

```

From the detailed agent logs (prior identical run same day, pod linuxptp-daemon-5mcqg):

```

[ptphelper.go: 123] GetProfileLogID attempt 1 failed for test-bc-master1_test-bc-master1: could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-5mcqg container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded, retrying...

[ptphelper.go: 123] GetProfileLogID attempt 2 failed for test-bc-master1_test-bc-master1: ... err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded, retrying...

[ptptesthelper.go: 68] GetClockIDForeign retry due to err: could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream ... err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded

```

Additionally, the API server was returning Unauthorized by 08:29 UTC (end of job), with the bc ClockSync tests running at 07:02–08:29 UTC — indicating progressive auth/credential degradation throughout the long 3.5-hour job:

```

error: You must be logged in to the server (Unauthorized)

E0624 08:29:47.118913 memcache.go:265] "Unhandled Error" err="couldn't get current server API group list: the server has asked for the client to provide credentials"

```

• Root Cause: The test helper GetProfileLogID in test/pkg/ptphelper/ptphelper.go opens a live Kubernetes log stream (Follow: true, no TailLines) via pods.GetPodLogsRegex searching for the regex (?m).?Ptp4lConf: #profile: (.|\n)?message_tag \[([^\]]*)\] within a 3-minute timeout. The stream — opened with context.TODO() so no test-side deadline — is being terminated by the infrastructure with context deadline exceeded before the regex can be matched. This error pattern repeats identically across all ClockSync tests in bc, dualfollower, dualnicbc, and dualnicbcha suites, confirming a cluster-wide log streaming connectivity issue rather than a daemon log format regression. The linuxptp-daemon does emit this log line (printNodeProfile in pkg/daemon/daemon.go:1369 writes the rendered Ptp4lConf with #profile: prefix and message_tag), but the HTTP connection carrying the pod log stream is dropped before the data reaches the test. The progressive Unauthorized failures at job end suggest the kubeconfig token was near expiry, which may also have disrupted API server proxy connections to node kubelets mid-run.
• Breaking PR/Commit:


TL;DR: Cluster log-stream collapse + API auth expiry wiped 22 of 24 tests; a linuxptp-daemon state-machine regression caused the remaining 2 TGM holdover failures.



Root Cause Groups:


• 12/24 failures — linuxptp-daemon log stream unreachable (ClockSync GetProfileLogID/GetClockIDForeign timeout): All ClockSync tests in bc (3), dualfollower (3), dualnicbc (3), dualnicbcha (2), and oc-serial test 1 (1) hit could not find regex in log stream … error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded or timedout waiting for matches. The test helper opens a live kubectl logs --follow stream and waits for a ptp4l profile or sync line that never arrives within 3–10 minutes. Two compounding factors: (a) ptp4l may not be reaching SLAVE state cluster-wide (no grandmaster reachable or log format changed by PR #613), and (b) when the API server began degrading, streaming connections were silently severed rather than cleanly refused.

• 10/24 failures — Kubernetes API server Unauthorized mid-run (oc-serial tests 2–11): After the first oc-serial test completed at ~07:29 UTC, the kubeconfig credentials expired or the API server rejected them (Unauthorized / couldn't get current server API group list: the server has asked for the client to provide credentials). Every subsequent test aborted immediately at the DaemonSet readiness check (linuxptp-daemon DaemonSet must exist before readiness wait → Unauthorized). One variant also showed dial tcp 10.8.34.117:6443: connection refused, indicating the API server briefly went fully unreachable.

• 2/24 failures — TGM holdover state-machine regression (tgm suite): Both T-GM holdover tests fail deterministically: disabling GNSS causes ts2phcState to be forced to PTP_FREERUN (not PTP_HOLDOVER) when NoSourceTSCount == 2 in linuxptp-daemon/pkg/event/event.go:402–403, making ClockClass 7 unreachable. Test 1 exhausted its 3-minute poll; test 2 observed map[metric:248] (freerun) throughout the 10-second sampling window. This is a deterministic regression, not a flake.


Cross-Suite Patterns:


PatternSuites affected
GetProfileLogID / GetClockIDForeign log stream timeoutbc, dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha, oc (test 1)
API server Unauthorized cascadeoc (tests 2–11)
GNSS → ClockClass 7 holdover state-machine bugtgm

The log-stream failures are 100% cross-suite (5 of 6 suites, identical error text, different pod names) — confirming a cluster-wide cause rather than a suite-specific issue. The oc Unauthorized failures are oc-specific but are a secondary consequence of the same cluster degradation event. The tgm failures are suite-specific and code-driven.


No suite is entirely clean. dualnicbcha is the only suite where the log-stream failure count is below its total suite size (2 of 2 failed; no "passing" baseline in that suite either).



Actionable Recommendations:


1. Re-run the job immediately and monitor the PTP grandmaster. The log-stream and API-auth failures bear all hallmarks of a transient lab infrastructure event (grandmaster unreachable, credential rotation, API server instability on telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com around 2026-06-24 07:00–08:30 UTC). A clean re-run will confirm whether the 22 non-TGM failures are infra flakes.

2. Audit linuxptp-daemon PR #613 (0459bc0, "upstream-sync-2026-06-22") for ptp4l log-format changes. If the upstream sync altered the format of the Ptp4lConf: or selected best master clock log lines, the test regexes in ptphelper.go and pods.go will never match regardless of cluster health. Compare the ptp4l log output from a pre-PR-613 build against a post-PR-613 pod to confirm or rule out a log-format regression.

3. Fix the TGM holdover state machine in event.go:402–403. Remove or guard the unconditional ts2phcState = PTP_FREERUN override when NoSourceTSCount == 2; instead, allow the state to transition to PTP_HOLDOVER so the existing ClockClass 7 branch at lines 459–461 and 430–433 becomes reachable. This is a deterministic code regression and will not self-heal with a re-run.


Overall Issue Class: infra_other (dominant — 22/24 failures) with an embedded test_regression (2/24 tgm failures from PR #613 state-machine change)


Overall Severity: CRITICAL — the entire 24-test job failed; the cluster experienced mid-run auth collapse, and a code regression in the TGM holdover path is deterministic and will recur on every re-run.

Actionable Recommendations:


1. Re-run the job immediately and monitor the PTP grandmaster. The log-stream and API-auth failures bear all hallmarks of a transient lab infrastructure event (grandmaster unreachable, credential rotation, API server instability on telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com around 2026-06-24 07:00–08:30 UTC). A clean re-run will confirm whether the 22 non-TGM failures are infra flakes.

2. Audit linuxptp-daemon PR #613 (0459bc0, "upstream-sync-2026-06-22") for ptp4l log-format changes. If the upstream sync altered the format of the Ptp4lConf: or selected best master clock log lines, the test regexes in ptphelper.go and pods.go will never match regardless of cluster health. Compare the ptp4l log output from a pre-PR-613 build against a post-PR-613 pod to confirm or rule out a log-format regression.

3. Fix the TGM holdover state machine in event.go:402–403. Remove or guard the unconditional ts2phcState = PTP_FREERUN override when NoSourceTSCount == 2; instead, allow the state to transition to PTP_HOLDOVER so the existing ClockClass 7 branch at lines 459–461 and 430–433 becomes reachable. This is a deterministic code regression and will not self-heal with a re-run.


Overall Issue Class: infra_other (dominant — 22/24 failures) with an embedded test_regression (2/24 tgm failures from PR #613 state-machine change)


Overall Severity: CRITICAL — the entire 24-test job failed; the cluster experienced mid-run auth collapse, and a code regression in the TGM holdover path is deterministic and will recur on every re-run.




Per-Suite Details



bc



Suite: bc (3 failures)


Suite TL;DR: All 3 ClockSync tests failed because Kubernetes log streaming to linuxptp-daemon pods returned context deadline exceeded / timeout errors during the GetProfileLogID step, preventing the test from locating the ptp4l config log line needed to derive the foreign master clock ID.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: Kubernetes pod log stream failure in GetProfileLogID (affects: all 3 tests — "Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master", "Can provide a profile with higher priority")
• Evidence:

```

could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-hrsj8 container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=timedout waiting for matches


could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-fpgx7 container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded

```

From the detailed agent logs (prior identical run same day, pod linuxptp-daemon-5mcqg):

```

[ptphelper.go: 123] GetProfileLogID attempt 1 failed for test-bc-master1_test-bc-master1: could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-5mcqg container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded, retrying...

[ptphelper.go: 123] GetProfileLogID attempt 2 failed for test-bc-master1_test-bc-master1: ... err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded, retrying...

[ptptesthelper.go: 68] GetClockIDForeign retry due to err: could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream ... err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded

```

Additionally, the API server was returning Unauthorized by 08:29 UTC (end of job), with the bc ClockSync tests running at 07:02–08:29 UTC — indicating progressive auth/credential degradation throughout the long 3.5-hour job:

```

error: You must be logged in to the server (Unauthorized)

E0624 08:29:47.118913 memcache.go:265] "Unhandled Error" err="couldn't get current server API group list: the server has asked for the client to provide credentials"

```

• Root Cause: The test helper GetProfileLogID in test/pkg/ptphelper/ptphelper.go opens a live Kubernetes log stream (Follow: true, no TailLines) via pods.GetPodLogsRegex searching for the regex (?m).?Ptp4lConf: #profile: (.|\n)?message_tag \[([^\]]*)\] within a 3-minute timeout. The stream — opened with context.TODO() so no test-side deadline — is being terminated by the infrastructure with context deadline exceeded before the regex can be matched. This error pattern repeats identically across all ClockSync tests in bc, dualfollower, dualnicbc, and dualnicbcha suites, confirming a cluster-wide log streaming connectivity issue rather than a daemon log format regression. The linuxptp-daemon does emit this log line (printNodeProfile in pkg/daemon/daemon.go:1369 writes the rendered Ptp4lConf with #profile: prefix and message_tag), but the HTTP connection carrying the pod log stream is dropped before the data reaches the test. The progressive Unauthorized failures at job end suggest the kubeconfig token was near expiry, which may also have disrupted API server proxy connections to node kubelets mid-run.
• Breaking PR/Commit:


TL;DR: Cluster log-stream collapse + API auth expiry wiped 22 of 24 tests; a linuxptp-daemon state-machine regression caused the remaining 2 TGM holdover failures.



Root Cause Groups:


• 12/24 failures — linuxptp-daemon log stream unreachable (ClockSync GetProfileLogID/GetClockIDForeign timeout): All ClockSync tests in bc (3), dualfollower (3), dualnicbc (3), dualnicbcha (2), and oc-serial test 1 (1) hit could not find regex in log stream … error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded or timedout waiting for matches. The test helper opens a live kubectl logs --follow stream and waits for a ptp4l profile or sync line that never arrives within 3–10 minutes. Two compounding factors: (a) ptp4l may not be reaching SLAVE state cluster-wide (no grandmaster reachable or log format changed by PR #613), and (b) when the API server began degrading, streaming connections were silently severed rather than cleanly refused.

• 10/24 failures — Kubernetes API server Unauthorized mid-run (oc-serial tests 2–11): After the first oc-serial test completed at ~07:29 UTC, the kubeconfig credentials expired or the API server rejected them (Unauthorized / couldn't get current server API group list: the server has asked for the client to provide credentials). Every subsequent test aborted immediately at the DaemonSet readiness check (linuxptp-daemon DaemonSet must exist before readiness wait → Unauthorized). One variant also showed dial tcp 10.8.34.117:6443: connection refused, indicating the API server briefly went fully unreachable.

• 2/24 failures — TGM holdover state-machine regression (tgm suite): Both T-GM holdover tests fail deterministically: disabling GNSS causes ts2phcState to be forced to PTP_FREERUN (not PTP_HOLDOVER) when NoSourceTSCount == 2 in linuxptp-daemon/pkg/event/event.go:402–403, making ClockClass 7 unreachable. Test 1 exhausted its 3-minute poll; test 2 observed map[metric:248] (freerun) throughout the 10-second sampling window. This is a deterministic regression, not a flake.


Cross-Suite Patterns:


PatternSuites affected
GetProfileLogID / GetClockIDForeign log stream timeoutbc, dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha, oc (test 1)
API server Unauthorized cascadeoc (tests 2–11)
GNSS → ClockClass 7 holdover state-machine bugtgm

The log-stream failures are 100% cross-suite (5 of 6 suites, identical error text, different pod names) — confirming a cluster-wide cause rather than a suite-specific issue. The oc Unauthorized failures are oc-specific but are a secondary consequence of the same cluster degradation event. The tgm failures are suite-specific and code-driven.


No suite is entirely clean. dualnicbcha is the only suite where the log-stream failure count is below its total suite size (2 of 2 failed; no "passing" baseline in that suite either).



Actionable Recommendations:


1. Re-run the job immediately and monitor the PTP grandmaster. The log-stream and API-auth failures bear all hallmarks of a transient lab infrastructure event (grandmaster unreachable, credential rotation, API server instability on telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com around 2026-06-24 07:00–08:30 UTC). A clean re-run will confirm whether the 22 non-TGM failures are infra flakes.

2. Audit linuxptp-daemon PR #613 (0459bc0, "upstream-sync-2026-06-22") for ptp4l log-format changes. If the upstream sync altered the format of the Ptp4lConf: or selected best master clock log lines, the test regexes in ptphelper.go and pods.go will never match regardless of cluster health. Compare the ptp4l log output from a pre-PR-613 build against a post-PR-613 pod to confirm or rule out a log-format regression.

3. Fix the TGM holdover state machine in event.go:402–403. Remove or guard the unconditional ts2phcState = PTP_FREERUN override when NoSourceTSCount == 2; instead, allow the state to transition to PTP_HOLDOVER so the existing ClockClass 7 branch at lines 459–461 and 430–433 becomes reachable. This is a deterministic code regression and will not self-heal with a re-run.


Overall Issue Class: infra_other (dominant — 22/24 failures) with an embedded test_regression (2/24 tgm failures from PR #613 state-machine change)


Overall Severity: CRITICAL — the entire 24-test job failed; the cluster experienced mid-run auth collapse, and a code regression in the TGM holdover path is deterministic and will recur on every re-run.

Suite TL;DR: All 3 ClockSync tests failed because Kubernetes log streaming to linuxptp-daemon pods returned context deadline exceeded / timeout errors during the GetProfileLogID step, preventing the test from locating the ptp4l config log line needed to derive the foreign master clock ID.


• Root Cause: The test helper GetProfileLogID in test/pkg/ptphelper/ptphelper.go opens a live Kubernetes log stream (Follow: true, no TailLines) via pods.GetPodLogsRegex searching for the regex (?m).?Ptp4lConf: #profile: (.|\n)?message_tag \[([^\]]*)\] within a 3-minute timeout. The stream — opened with context.TODO() so no test-side deadline — is being terminated by the infrastructure with context deadline exceeded before the regex can be matched. This error pattern repeats identically across all ClockSync tests in bc, dualfollower, dualnicbc, and dualnicbcha suites, confirming a cluster-wide log streaming connectivity issue rather than a daemon log format regression. The linuxptp-daemon does emit this log line (printNodeProfile in pkg/daemon/daemon.go:1369 writes the rendered Ptp4lConf with #profile: prefix and message_tag), but the HTTP connection carrying the pod log stream is dropped before the data reaches the test. The progressive Unauthorized failures at job end suggest the kubeconfig token was near expiry, which may also have disrupted API server proxy connections to node kubelets mid-run.
• Breaking PR/Commit:



Cross-Suite Summary: periodic-ci-openshift-release-main-nightly-4.19-e2e-telco5g-ptp


Job: periodic-ci-openshift-release-main-nightly-4.19-e2e-telco5g-ptp | Version: 4.19 | Total failures: 21



TL;DR: 19 of 21 failures trace to progressive API server credential expiry; 2 are a likely hardware flake in the TGM suite.



Root Cause Groups:


• 19/21 failures — Kubeconfig credential expiry / API server degradation (suites: bc, dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha)

The ~3.5-hour serial run exhausted the cluster's short-lived service-account token. Degradation progressed in three observable stages across suites:

1. Early degradation (dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha): Kubernetes log-stream connections to linuxptp-daemon-* pods dropped with error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded or timedout waiting for matches, preventing the test helper (ptphelper.go:631/811) from confirming PTP sync via regex match on container log output.
2. Full TCP unreachability (bc, first failure): The control-plane VIP 10.8.34.117:6443 became TCP-unreachable mid-suite (dial tcp … i/o timeout), timing out after 573 s.
3. Hard 401 Unauthorized cascade (bc, 9 remaining + post-test gather): After the VIP came back, every API call was rejected with Unauthorized — consistent with a control-plane restart triggering token rotation — cascading through all remaining bc tests and all post-test artifact collection steps.

• 2/21 failures — WPC T-GM DPLL never entered HOLDOVER within test window (suite: tgm, suite-specific)

The first tgm test ("Testing WPC T-GM holdover through connection loss") timed out after ~59.5 minutes waiting for clock class 7. The dpll.go:650–674 state machine requires hardware DPLL state 4 (DPLL_HOLDOVER) before setting clock class 7; that transition never occurred within the polling window, exhausting the suite budget and preventing the second test from running at all (pure cascade, A suite timeout occurred). A known TODO at dpll.go:656 flags instability in this GNSS holdover path. No daemon logs from the failing run are available to distinguish a hardware/timing flake from a daemon regression.



Cross-Suite Patterns:


Root Causebcdualfollowerdualnicbcdualnicbchatgm
API credential expiry / log-stream timeout✓ (11)✓ (3)✓ (3)✓ (2)—
TGM DPLL holdover hardware/timing failure————✓ (2)

The API credential failure is fully cross-suite (4 of 5 suites). The context deadline exceeded log-stream pattern appears in identical form across dualfollower, dualnicbc, and dualnicbcha on different daemon pods (vzwc2, fv8pz, 2sffq, 4nzrs, 2h2w9), confirming it is a cluster-wide event and not pod-specific. The bc suite experienced the most severe form (full TCP timeout then 401 cascade) because it ran later in the job when credential state was furthest degraded. The tgm failure is suite-specific and unrelated to credentials — it involves hardware GNSS/DPLL behavior on cnfdf30/31/32.



Actionable Recommendations:


1. Re-run the job immediately. All 19 API-credential failures are consistent flakes caused by token expiry mid-run; a clean re-run on a freshly provisioned cluster (fresh credentials) should clear them. If the same Unauthorized cascade recurs, investigate the ptpcimno cluster's kubeconfig refresh mechanism and whether the telco5g CI credential vault rotates tokens during the run window.

2. Fix credential lifetime / mid-run refresh for long serial runs. The root cause is structural: a ~3.5-hour serial suite that outlives the service-account token TTL. Either (a) increase the token TTL for the CI service account on ptpcimno, (b) implement a credential refresh step between serial suites, or (c) break up the serial suite chain so each segment gets a fresh kubeconfig. The dualfollower/dualnicbc/dualnicbcha degradation before the hard bc TCP failure suggests the window of failure is predictable and fixable.

3. Capture linuxptp-daemon logs for the tgm holdover test on the next run. The 2 tgm failures cannot be classified as flake vs. regression without daemon output showing the DPLL state transitions. Add log capture for linuxptp-daemon pods on cnfdf30/31/32 nodes scoped to the holdover test window. If the DPLL never emits state 4, the issue is the GNSS simulation setup or hardware; if it emits state 4 but clock class 7 is still not set, investigate the isInSpecOffsetInRange() check at dpll.go:868 and the known tech-debt at dpll.go:656.


Overall Issue Class: infra_timeout


Overall Severity: HIGH — 19/21 failures are a systemic infra credential issue that will repeat every long run; the tgm path has a known flaky hardware dependency that blocks that suite entirely when it occurs.




Per-Suite Details



bc



Suite: bc (11 failures)


Suite TL;DR: All 11 bc-suite failures stem from a cluster-level API server disruption mid-run — the control plane became unreachable (i/o timeout) and then began rejecting credentials (HTTP 401 Unauthorized), cascading into every subsequent test timing out.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: API Server TCP Unreachable (affects: Slave can sync to master)
• Evidence:

```

finding pod for test-bc-master1_test-bc-master1: Get

"https://10.8.34.117:6443/api/v1/namespaces/openshift-ptp/pods?labelSelector=app%3Dlinuxptp-daemon":

dial tcp 10.8.34.117:6443: i/o timeout

```

This test timed out after 573.498 s — it kept retrying until it hit the Ginkgo deadline.

• Root Cause: The API server endpoint at 10.8.34.117:6443 (a VIP or floating-IP for the control plane, distinct from the master node IPs 10.8.34.105/106/110 listed in oc get nodes) became TCP-unreachable mid-test. The test helper GetForeignClockID (calls into ptphelper.go, line ~631) polls pod logs via the API; with no API connectivity it spins until timeout. No cluster node was in NotReady state when the suite started, so this is a transient control-plane disruption that began after the suite was already running.
• Breaking PR/Commit: Unknown — infra disruption, not a code change.
• Is it a flake? Yes
• Suggested Fix: No code change needed; re-run the job. If this IP (10.8.34.117) recurs, verify the telco5g API VIP health and the control-plane load balancer for the ptpcimno cluster.

• Group 2: API Server Returning HTTP 401 Unauthorized (affects: 9 remaining tests + suite timeout)
• Evidence:

```

linuxptp-daemon DaemonSet must exist before readiness wait

Expected success, but got an error:

<*errors.StatusError | 0xc000999720>:

Unauthorized

...

Status: "Failure",

Message: "Unauthorized

```

Repeated identically for 9 consecutive tests. Post-test artifact gather confirms the same state:

```

error: You must be logged in to the server (Unauthorized)

error running backup collection: the server has asked for the client to provide credentials

creating temp namespace: Unauthorized

```

And from the step entrypoint:

```

error: You must be logged in to the server (Unauthorized)

{"component":"entrypoint","error":"wrapped process failed: exit status 1",...}

```

• Root Cause: After the control plane disruption in Group 1, every subsequent test fails at its first API call — checking whether the linuxptp-daemon DaemonSet exists in openshift-ptp. The cluster API came back but is rejecting the KUBECONFIG credentials (short-lived service-account token or client certificate was invalidated, e.g., due to a control-plane restart triggering token rotation). All 9 tests hit their 300 s Ginkgo timeout doing this single failing call. The final test receives A suite timeout occurred because the 1-hour suite timer expired. The artifact-gathering pods (must-gather, gather-extra, gather-audit-logs) all fail with the same Unauthorized error, confirming cluster-wide credential invalidation.
• Breaking PR/Commit: Unknown — infra disruption, not a code change.
• Is it a flake? Yes
• Suggested Fix: No code change needed; re-run the job. The test harness could be improved by detecting HTTP 401 at the WaitForDaemonSetReadiness stage and failing fast with a clearer infra-error message instead of spinning for 300 s.

Suite Issue Class: infra_other

Suite Severity: HIGH


dualfollower



Suite: dualfollower (3 failures)


Suite TL;DR: All 3 dualfollower tests failed because the Kubernetes API log-streaming connection to linuxptp-daemon-vzwc2 dropped mid-test with context deadline exceeded, a symptom of cluster credential expiry that progressed to full Unauthorized rejections by the time later suites (bc-serial) ran.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: Kubernetes API log-stream connection failure due to credential expiry (affects: all 3 — "Slave can sync to master", "Dual follower can sync when one follower port goes down", "Can provide a profile with higher priority")
• Evidence:

```

could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-vzwc2 container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded

```

(tests 1 & 2, ~202–206s timeout)

```

could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-vzwc2 container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=timedout waiting for matches

```

(test 3, ~204s timeout)


From ci-operator-build-log.txt post-test steps:

```

error: You must be logged in to the server (Unauthorized)

creating temp namespace: Unauthorized

error getting cluster version: Unauthorized

E0702 08:31:18.720224 memcache.go:265 "Unhandled Error" err="couldn't get current server API group list: the server has asked for the client to provide credentials"

```


From junit-failures.txt, the bc-serial suite (which ran after dualfollower) shows full API rejection:

```

finding pod for test-bc-master1_test-bc-master1: Get "https://10.8.34.117:6443/api/v1/namespaces/openshift-ptp/pods?...": dial tcp 10.8.34.117:6443: i/o timeout

```

and repeated Unauthorized failures for every single bc-serial test.


• Root Cause: The telco5g test cluster's kubeconfig token expired partway through the ~3.5-hour test run. During the dualfollower suite, the credential degradation manifested as the Kubernetes log-streaming API (GetLogs with follow) dropping connections with context deadline exceeded before the test's regex (looking for a foreign master clock ID line in linuxptp-daemon-container) was found. This is not a PTP sync failure — the daemon pod linuxptp-daemon-vzwc2 was never confirmed unhealthy; the test framework simply lost its authenticated connection to the API server's log stream endpoint. The same context deadline exceeded pattern is present in the dualnicbc-serial and dualnicbcha-serial suites across different pods (linuxptp-daemon-fv8pz, linuxptp-daemon-2sffq, linuxptp-daemon-4nzrs), confirming this is a cluster-wide infra event, not pod-specific. The relevant test helper is ptphelper.go around lines 631/811 (per investigation-summary.txt), which streams container logs and scans for the profile regex with a ~200s context deadline.

• Breaking PR/Commit: Unknown — this is a transient infrastructure credential expiry, not a code regression. No code change in release-4.19 is implicated.

• Is it a flake? Yes — this is an infrastructure flake caused by kubeconfig token expiry on the telco5g bare-metal cluster mid-run; a retry of the job with fresh credentials should pass.

• Suggested Fix: Investigate why the telco5g cluster kubeconfig token expires mid-job (the job ran from ~05:01Z to ~08:31Z, about 3.5 hours); either rotate tokens with a longer TTL or have the setup step refresh credentials before each test suite begins.

Suite Issue Class: infra_timeout

Suite Severity: MEDIUM


dualnicbc



Suite: dualnicbc (3 failures)


Suite TL;DR: All 3 ClockSync tests timed out waiting for linuxptp-daemon log lines confirming PTP clock sync, with the streaming connection itself dying with context deadline exceeded — a pattern mirrored across 3 other suites in the same run, pointing to cluster-wide API server instability rather than a product regression.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: Kubernetes API log-stream context deadline / regex not found (affects: all 3 — "Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master", "Can provide a profile with higher priority")
• Evidence:

```

could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for

openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-fv8pz container=linuxptp-daemon-container,

err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded

```

```

could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for

openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-2sffq container=linuxptp-daemon-container,

err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded

```

```

could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for

openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-fv8pz container=linuxptp-daemon-container,

err=timedout waiting for matches

```

Cross-suite confirmation: the bc-serial suite (which ran in the same job) shows dial tcp 10.8.34.117:6443: i/o timeout on the API server, and all post-test gather steps fail with Unauthorized. The ci-operator build log confirms: error: You must be logged in to the server (Unauthorized) at 2026-07-02T08:31:04Z, with creating temp namespace: Unauthorized in must-gather.

• Root Cause: The test helper (referenced as ptphelper.go:631 and ptphelper.go:811 in the investigation-summary) streams pod logs via the Kubernetes API to match a regex confirming a foreign clock ID in ptp4l output. Two of the three tests fail because the HTTP streaming connection to the API server was dropped mid-read (context deadline exceeded); the third fails because the stream connected but the expected log line never appeared (timedout waiting for matches). Both failure modes are consistent with a degraded or overloaded API server — corroborated by bc-serial's hard network timeout (i/o timeout to the same API server VIP) and the total Unauthorized state after test completion. The OCP 4.19 nightly used was 4.19.0-0.nightly-2026-07-01-085304.
• Breaking PR/Commit: Unknown — no product-side code change could be identified. The local clones of linuxptp-daemon and cloud-event-proxy only carry main branches (not release-4.19); check_backport.py was not present to verify backport status. The most recent main-only merges (fd6ff1e in linuxptp-daemon, 0907127 / "Fix E1-aware E3 guard race in T-BC profiles" in cloud-event-proxy) are on main only and cannot be the root cause for this release-4.19 job unless cherry-picked.
• Is it a flake? Yes — the simultaneous failure of log streaming across 8+ tests in 3 independent suites (dualnicbc, dualfollower, dualnicbcha), combined with the bc-serial network timeout and Unauthorized post-test state, is consistent with a transient cluster-level API server event, not a deterministic product regression.
• Suggested Fix: Re-run the periodic job against the same or next nightly to confirm whether failures persist. If they recur, examine the release-4.19 linuxptp-daemon pod logs directly (once the cluster is stable) to determine whether ptp4l is producing the expected profile/foreign-clock-ID log lines after sync.

Suite Issue Class: infra_timeout

Suite Severity: MEDIUM


dualnicbcha



Suite: dualnicbcha (2 failures)


Suite TL;DR: Both ClockSync te

2026-07-02T05:00
❓ infra
❌ failure4.204.20-e2e-telco5g-ptp168mtest_regression HIGH
11/13 failures are a test/daemon version mismatch; 2/13 are a T-GM hardware holdover regression.
13 failed tests
INFRA LOW
🆕 New issue
Full AI Analysis

TL;DR: 11/13 failures are a test/daemon version mismatch; 2/13 are a T-GM hardware holdover regression.



Root Cause Groups:


• 11/13 failures — Ptp4lConf: #profile: log line absent from release-4.20 daemon (bc: 3, dualfollower: 3, dualnicbc: 3, dualnicbcha: 2)

The test helper GetProfileLogID (test code running from main) searches for a log regex (Ptp4lConf: #profile: ) that is only emitted after *configInput = configOutput + printNodeProfile — code introduced in linuxptp-daemon main PR #617 (commit fd6ff1e, merged 2026-06-30) and not backported to release-4.20. The CI job deploys release-4.20 daemon images (USE_UPSTREAM=false), so the pattern is never logged. GetProfileLogID's internal 3-minute deadline consumes the entire outer Eventually window, leaving the /var/run fallback no time to run. All failures surface as either timedout waiting for matches (stream alive, pattern never appeared) or context deadline exceeded (Kubernetes log-stream dropped), with a secondary cluster-API Unauthorized state observed post-run in dualnicbc/dualnicbcha, consistent with infrastructure stress from 11 concurrent 200-second stream-read timeouts.


• 2/13 failures — T-GM DPLL does not enter holdover on GNSS disruption (tgm: 2)

When GNSS signal is lost or rebooted on WPC T-GM hardware, the Intel DPLL is expected to transition to lockStatus: holdover (clock class 7). Instead, the DPLL stays in or falls directly to FREERUN (clock class 248), so openshift_ptp_clock_class never reaches 7. Both tgm tests fail: one by timeout polling for clock class 7, one by direct metric assertion finding 248. This is independent of the log-line issue; the release-4.20 daemon or underlying firmware may have a regression in holdover state management.



Cross-Suite Patterns:


Root CauseSuites AffectedFailures
#profile: log line missing from release-4.20 daemonbc, dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha11
T-GM DPLL does not enter holdover (clock class 7)tgm2

The 11-failure group is fully cross-suite: all four PTP topology suites hit identical pod-log-stream errors on six different pods across different nodes, ruling out any single pod, node, or suite-specific cause. The tgm failures are completely isolated to hardware GNSS/holdover behavior and share no mechanism with the other 11.



Actionable Recommendations:


1. Backport linuxptp-daemon main PR #617 to release-4.20 (fixes 11/13 failures). The commit fd6ff1e adds *configInput = configOutput and the unconditional printNodeProfile call that emits Ptp4lConf: #profile: into daemon logs. Without this, any main test branch that relies on GetProfileLogID will always time out against release-4.20 images. Alternatively, gate the test's log-search path on daemon version and immediately fall through to the /var/run exec-based fallback instead of waiting a full 3 minutes first.

2. Investigate T-GM DPLL holdover regression on release-4.20 (fixes 2/13 failures). Audit recent commits to linuxptp-daemon/release-4.20 and cloud-event-proxy/release-4.20 for changes in pkg/event/event.go DPLL state-transition logic. Also verify WPC hardware firmware version matches the expected holdover capability; if firmware is the variable, pin firmware version in the CI telco5g cluster.

3. Extend the outer Eventually timeout in BasicClockSyncCheck or make the /var/run fallback the first path (not the last resort). The current design serially exhausts a 3-minute log-stream deadline before trying a fast exec-based config read, meaning any transient log-streaming disruption (or version mismatch) guarantees test failure regardless of actual PTP sync state.


Overall Issue Class: test_regression

(The dominant failure mode — 11/13 — is caused by main test code depending on a daemon log line that exists only in main, not in the release-4.20 images the job is actually testing. The tgm pair is test_failure / potential daemon regression, but does not change the overall classification.)


Overall Severity: HIGH

(11/13 failures are systemic and reproducible across every topology suite in every run that pairs main test scripts with release-4.20 daemon images; the job cannot pass in this configuration without the backport or a test-side workaround.)




Per-Suite Details



bc



Suite: bc (3 failures)


Suite TL;DR: All 3 BC ClockSync tests time out because GetProfileLogID cannot find the Ptp4lConf: #profile: log pattern in the release-4.20 linuxptp-daemon container, and the /var/run fallback is exhausted within the 3-minute Eventually window.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: GetProfileLogID log pattern absent from release-4.20 linuxptp-daemon (affects: all 3 tests — "Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master", "Can provide a profile with higher priority")
• Evidence:

```

could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-g59s2 container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded

```

```

could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-7khz9 container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded

```

```

could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-g59s2 container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=timedout waiting for matches

```

The same error occurs across all four suites (bc, dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha) on different pods (-g59s2, -7khz9, -68lc9, -d75dq), confirming a daemon-wide, systematic failure.


• Root Cause: GetClockIDForeign (called from BasicClockSyncCheck in test/pkg/ptptesthelper/ptptesthelper.go:66) first calls GetProfileLogID (test/pkg/ptphelper/ptphelper.go:84), which searches for the regex (?m).?Ptp4lConf: #profile: (.|\n)?message_tag \[(.)\] in the linuxptp-daemon-container logs. This pattern is only emitted when the linuxptp-daemon calls printNodeProfile(nodeProfile) after configInput = configOutput (linuxptp-daemon/pkg/daemon/daemon.go:1152-1154) writes the rendered config (containing #profile: ) back into nodeProfile.Ptp4lConf. Both the *configInput = configOutput assignment and the unconditional printNodeProfile call at line 1412 were introduced in linuxptp-daemon main PR #617 (commit fd6ff1e, merged June 30 2026), but have not been backported to release-4.20. The CI job runs test scripts from ptp-operator main against release-4.20 production images, so the daemon never emits the expected log line. The /var/run-based fallback (GetConfigForProfileFromVarRun, added in ptp-operator main commit 84723c8 June 22) is invoked only after GetProfileLogID's own 3-minute deadline elapses (~t=200s), which coincides with the outer Eventually timeout (3 min), leaving insufficient time to complete exec commands inside the pod and verify the result. GetClockIDForeign then returns lastErr from the log scan, and Eventually reports failure at ~202s.

• Breaking PR/Commit: linuxptp-daemon main PR #617 (fd6ff1e, June 30 2026) added *configInput = configOutput and the unconditional printNodeProfile that the test requires — but this has not been cherry-picked to release-4.20. Per branch-awareness policy, a main-only change cannot be the root cause; the true underlying issue is that the main test code (GetProfileLogID, introduced in ptp-operator commit 73a75ec, March 25 2026) was written assuming a log-output behavior that exists only on main linuxptp-daemon, not in release-4.20 images. Secondary contributing commit: ptp-operator bb43e35 (June 23 2026) has prerequisite k8snetworkplumbingwg/linuxptp-daemon#215 which also affects daemon startup behavior



TL;DR: 11/13 failures are a test/daemon version mismatch; 2/13 are a T-GM hardware holdover regression.



Root Cause Groups:


• 11/13 failures — Ptp4lConf: #profile: log line absent from release-4.20 daemon (bc: 3, dualfollower: 3, dualnicbc: 3, dualnicbcha: 2)

The test helper GetProfileLogID (test code running from main) searches for a log regex (Ptp4lConf: #profile: ) that is only emitted after *configInput = configOutput + printNodeProfile — code introduced in linuxptp-daemon main PR #617 (commit fd6ff1e, merged 2026-06-30) and not backported to release-4.20. The CI job deploys release-4.20 daemon images (USE_UPSTREAM=false), so the pattern is never logged. GetProfileLogID's internal 3-minute deadline consumes the entire outer Eventually window, leaving the /var/run fallback no time to run. All failures surface as either timedout waiting for matches (stream alive, pattern never appeared) or context deadline exceeded (Kubernetes log-stream dropped), with a secondary cluster-API Unauthorized state observed post-run in dualnicbc/dualnicbcha, consistent with infrastructure stress from 11 concurrent 200-second stream-read timeouts.


• 2/13 failures — T-GM DPLL does not enter holdover on GNSS disruption (tgm: 2)

When GNSS signal is lost or rebooted on WPC T-GM hardware, the Intel DPLL is expected to transition to lockStatus: holdover (clock class 7). Instead, the DPLL stays in or falls directly to FREERUN (clock class 248), so openshift_ptp_clock_class never reaches 7. Both tgm tests fail: one by timeout polling for clock class 7, one by direct metric assertion finding 248. This is independent of the log-line issue; the release-4.20 daemon or underlying firmware may have a regression in holdover state management.



Cross-Suite Patterns:


Root CauseSuites AffectedFailures
#profile: log line missing from release-4.20 daemonbc, dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha11
T-GM DPLL does not enter holdover (clock class 7)tgm2

The 11-failure group is fully cross-suite: all four PTP topology suites hit identical pod-log-stream errors on six different pods across different nodes, ruling out any single pod, node, or suite-specific cause. The tgm failures are completely isolated to hardware GNSS/holdover behavior and share no mechanism with the other 11.



Actionable Recommendations:


1. Backport linuxptp-daemon main PR #617 to release-4.20 (fixes 11/13 failures). The commit fd6ff1e adds *configInput = configOutput and the unconditional printNodeProfile call that emits Ptp4lConf: #profile: into daemon logs. Without this, any main test branch that relies on GetProfileLogID will always time out against release-4.20 images. Alternatively, gate the test's log-search path on daemon version and immediately fall through to the /var/run exec-based fallback instead of waiting a full 3 minutes first.

2. Investigate T-GM DPLL holdover regression on release-4.20 (fixes 2/13 failures). Audit recent commits to linuxptp-daemon/release-4.20 and cloud-event-proxy/release-4.20 for changes in pkg/event/event.go DPLL state-transition logic. Also verify WPC hardware firmware version matches the expected holdover capability; if firmware is the variable, pin firmware version in the CI telco5g cluster.

3. Extend the outer Eventually timeout in BasicClockSyncCheck or make the /var/run fallback the first path (not the last resort). The current design serially exhausts a 3-minute log-stream deadline before trying a fast exec-based config read, meaning any transient log-streaming disruption (or version mismatch) guarantees test failure regardless of actual PTP sync state.


Overall Issue Class: test_regression

(The dominant failure mode — 11/13 — is caused by main test code depending on a daemon log line that exists only in main, not in the release-4.20 images the job is actually testing. The tgm pair is test_failure / potential daemon regression, but does not change the overall classification.)


Overall Severity: HIGH

(11/13 failures are systemic and reproducible across every topology suite in every run that pairs main test scripts with release-4.20 daemon images; the job cannot pass in this configuration without the backport or a test-side workaround.)

Instead, the DPLL stays in or falls directly to FREERUN (clock class 248), so openshift_ptp_clock_class never reaches 7. Both tgm tests fail: one by timeout polling for clock class 7, one by direct metric assertion finding 248. This is independent of the log-line issue; the release-4.20 daemon or underlying firmware may have a regression in holdover state management.



Cross-Suite Patterns:


Root CauseSuites AffectedFailures
#profile: log line missing from release-4.20 daemonbc, dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha11
T-GM DPLL does not enter holdover (clock class 7)tgm2

The 11-failure group is fully cross-suite: all four PTP topology suites hit identical pod-log-stream errors on six different pods across different nodes, ruling out any single pod, node, or suite-specific cause. The tgm failures are completely isolated to hardware GNSS/holdover behavior and share no mechanism with the other 11.



Actionable Recommendations:


1. Backport linuxptp-daemon main PR #617 to release-4.20 (fixes 11/13 failures). The commit fd6ff1e adds *configInput = configOutput and the unconditional printNodeProfile call that emits Ptp4lConf: #profile: into daemon logs. Without this, any main test branch that relies on GetProfileLogID will always time out against release-4.20 images. Alternatively, gate the test's log-search path on daemon version and immediately fall through to the /var/run exec-based fallback instead of waiting a full 3 minutes first.

2. Investigate T-GM DPLL holdover regression on release-4.20 (fixes 2/13 failures). Audit recent commits to linuxptp-daemon/release-4.20 and cloud-event-proxy/release-4.20 for changes in pkg/event/event.go DPLL state-transition logic. Also verify WPC hardware firmware version matches the expected holdover capability; if firmware is the variable, pin firmware version in the CI telco5g cluster.

3. Extend the outer Eventually timeout in BasicClockSyncCheck or make the /var/run fallback the first path (not the last resort). The current design serially exhausts a 3-minute log-stream deadline before trying a fast exec-based config read, meaning any transient log-streaming disruption (or version mismatch) guarantees test failure regardless of actual PTP sync state.


Overall Issue Class: test_regression

(The dominant failure mode — 11/13 — is caused by main test code depending on a daemon log line that exists only in main, not in the release-4.20 images the job is actually testing. The tgm pair is test_failure / potential daemon regression, but does not change the overall classification.)


Overall Severity: HIGH

(11/13 failures are systemic and reproducible across every topology suite in every run that pairs main test scripts with release-4.20 daemon images; the job cannot pass in this configuration without the backport or a test-side workaround.)




Per-Suite Details



bc



Suite: bc (3 failures)


Suite TL;DR: All 3 BC ClockSync tests time out because GetProfileLogID cannot find the Ptp4lConf: #profile: log pattern in the release-4.20 linuxptp-daemon container, and the /var/run fallback is exhausted within the 3-minute Eventually window.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: GetProfileLogID log pattern absent from release-4.20 linuxptp-daemon (affects: all 3 tests — "Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master", "Can provide a profile with higher priority")
• Evidence:

```

could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-g59s2 container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded

```

```

could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-7khz9 container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded

```

```

could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-g59s2 container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=timedout waiting for matches

```

The same error occurs across all four suites (bc, dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha) on different pods (-g59s2, -7khz9, -68lc9, -d75dq), confirming a daemon-wide, systematic failure.


• Root Cause: GetClockIDForeign (called from BasicClockSyncCheck in test/pkg/ptptesthelper/ptptesthelper.go:66) first calls GetProfileLogID (test/pkg/ptphelper/ptphelper.go:84), which searches for the regex (?m).?Ptp4lConf: #profile: (.|\n)?message_tag \[(.)\] in the linuxptp-daemon-container logs. This pattern is only emitted when the linuxptp-daemon calls printNodeProfile(nodeProfile) after configInput = configOutput (linuxptp-daemon/pkg/daemon/daemon.go:1152-1154) writes the rendered config (containing #profile: ) back into nodeProfile.Ptp4lConf. Both the *configInput = configOutput assignment and the unconditional printNodeProfile call at line 1412 were introduced in linuxptp-daemon main PR #617 (commit fd6ff1e, merged June 30 2026), but have not been backported to release-4.20. The CI job runs test scripts from ptp-operator main against release-4.20 production images, so the daemon never emits the expected log line. The /var/run-based fallback (GetConfigForProfileFromVarRun, added in ptp-operator main commit 84723c8 June 22) is invoked only after GetProfileLogID's own 3-minute deadline elapses (~t=200s), which coincides with the outer Eventually timeout (3 min), leaving insufficient time to complete exec commands inside the pod and verify the result. GetClockIDForeign then returns lastErr from the log scan, and Eventually reports failure at ~202s.

• Breaking PR/Commit: linuxptp-daemon main PR #617 (fd6ff1e, June 30 2026) added *configInput = configOutput and the unconditional printNodeProfile that the test requires — but this has not been cherry-picked to release-4.20. Per branch-awareness policy, a main-only change cannot be the root cause; the true underlying issue is that the main test code (GetProfileLogID, introduced in ptp-operator commit 73a75ec, March 25 2026) was written assuming a log-output behavior that exists only on main linuxptp-daemon, not in release-4.20 images. Secondary contributing commit: ptp-operator bb43e35 (June 23 2026) has prerequisite k8snetworkplumbingwg/linuxptp-daemon#215 which also affects daemon startup behavior



TL;DR: 11/13 failures are a test/daemon version mismatch; 2/13 are a T-GM hardware holdover regression.



Root Cause Groups:


• 11/13 failures — Ptp4lConf: #profile: log line absent from release-4.20 daemon (bc: 3, dualfollower: 3, dualnicbc: 3, dualnicbcha: 2)

The test helper GetProfileLogID (test code running from main) searches for a log regex (Ptp4lConf: #profile: ) that is only emitted after *configInput = configOutput + printNodeProfile — code introduced in linuxptp-daemon main PR #617 (commit fd6ff1e, merged 2026-06-30) and not backported to release-4.20. The CI job deploys release-4.20 daemon images (USE_UPSTREAM=false), so the pattern is never logged. GetProfileLogID's internal 3-minute deadline consumes the entire outer Eventually window, leaving the /var/run fallback no time to run. All failures surface as either timedout waiting for matches (stream alive, pattern never appeared) or context deadline exceeded (Kubernetes log-stream dropped), with a secondary cluster-API Unauthorized state observed post-run in dualnicbc/dualnicbcha, consistent with infrastructure stress from 11 concurrent 200-second stream-read timeouts.


• 2/13 failures — T-GM DPLL does not enter holdover on GNSS disruption (tgm: 2)

When GNSS signal is lost or rebooted on WPC T-GM hardware, the Intel DPLL is expected to transition to lockStatus: holdover (clock class 7). Instead, the DPLL stays in or falls directly to FREERUN (clock class 248), so openshift_ptp_clock_class never reaches 7. Both tgm tests fail: one by timeout polling for clock class 7, one by direct metric assertion finding 248. This is independent of the log-line issue; the release-4.20 daemon or underlying firmware may have a regression in holdover state management.



Cross-Suite Patterns:


Root CauseSuites AffectedFailures
#profile: log line missing from release-4.20 daemonbc, dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha11
T-GM DPLL does not enter holdover (clock class 7)tgm2

The 11-failure group is fully cross-suite: all four PTP topology suites hit identical pod-log-stream errors on six different pods across different nodes, ruling out any single pod, node, or suite-specific cause. The tgm failures are completely isolated to hardware GNSS/holdover behavior and share no mechanism with the other 11.



Actionable Recommendations:


1. Backport linuxptp-daemon main PR #617 to release-4.20 (fixes 11/13 failures). The commit fd6ff1e adds *configInput = configOutput and the unconditional printNodeProfile call that emits Ptp4lConf: #profile: into daemon logs. Without this, any main test branch that relies on GetProfileLogID will always time out against release-4.20 images. Alternatively, gate the test's log-search path on daemon version and immediately fall through to the /var/run exec-based fallback instead of waiting a full 3 minutes first.

2. Investigate T-GM DPLL holdover regression on release-4.20 (fixes 2/13 failures). Audit recent commits to linuxptp-daemon/release-4.20 and cloud-event-proxy/release-4.20 for changes in pkg/event/event.go DPLL state-transition logic. Also verify WPC hardware firmware version matches the expected holdover capability; if firmware is the variable, pin firmware version in the CI telco5g cluster.

3. Extend the outer Eventually timeout in BasicClockSyncCheck or make the /var/run fallback the first path (not the last resort). The current design serially exhausts a 3-minute log-stream deadline before trying a fast exec-based config read, meaning any transient log-streaming disruption (or version mismatch) guarantees test failure regardless of actual PTP sync state.


Overall Issue Class: test_regression

(The dominant failure mode — 11/13 — is caused by main test code depending on a daemon log line that exists only in main, not in the release-4.20 images the job is actually testing. The tgm pair is test_failure / potential daemon regression, but does not change the overall classification.)


Overall Severity: HIGH

(11/13 failures are systemic and reproducible across every topology suite in every run that pairs main test scripts with release-4.20 daemon images; the job cannot pass in this configuration without the backport or a test-side workaround.)

All failures surface as either timedout waiting for matches (stream alive, pattern never appeared) or context deadline exceeded (Kubernetes log-stream dropped), with a secondary cluster-API Unauthorized state observed post-run in dualnicbc/dualnicbcha, consistent with infrastructure stress from 11 concurrent 200-second stream-read timeouts.


• 2/13 failures — T-GM DPLL does not enter holdover on GNSS disruption (tgm: 2)

When GNSS signal is lost or rebooted on WPC T-GM hardware, the Intel DPLL is expected to transition to lockStatus: holdover (clock class 7). Instead, the DPLL stays in or falls directly to FREERUN (clock class 248), so openshift_ptp_clock_class never reaches 7. Both tgm tests fail: one by timeout polling for clock class 7, one by direct metric assertion finding 248. This is independent of the log-line issue; the release-4.20 daemon or underlying firmware may have a regression in holdover state management.



Cross-Suite Patterns:


Root CauseSuites AffectedFailures
#profile: log line missing from release-4.20 daemonbc, dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha11
T-GM DPLL does not enter holdover (clock class 7)tgm2

The 11-failure group is fully cross-suite: all four PTP topology suites hit identical pod-log-stream errors on six different pods across different nodes, ruling out any single pod, node, or suite-specific cause. The tgm failures are completely isolated to hardware GNSS/holdover behavior and share no mechanism with the other 11.



Actionable Recommendations:


1. Backport linuxptp-daemon main PR #617 to release-4.20 (fixes 11/13 failures). The commit fd6ff1e adds *configInput = configOutput and the unconditional printNodeProfile call that emits Ptp4lConf: #profile: into daemon logs. Without this, any main test branch that relies on GetProfileLogID will always time out against release-4.20 images. Alternatively, gate the test's log-search path on daemon version and immediately fall through to the /var/run exec-based fallback instead of waiting a full 3 minutes first.

2. Investigate T-GM DPLL holdover regression on release-4.20 (fixes 2/13 failures). Audit recent commits to linuxptp-daemon/release-4.20 and cloud-event-proxy/release-4.20 for changes in pkg/event/event.go DPLL state-transition logic. Also verify WPC hardware firmware version matches the expected holdover capability; if firmware is the variable, pin firmware version in the CI telco5g cluster.

3. Extend the outer Eventually timeout in BasicClockSyncCheck or make the /var/run fallback the first path (not the last resort). The current design serially exhausts a 3-minute log-stream deadline before trying a fast exec-based config read, meaning any transient log-streaming disruption (or version mismatch) guarantees test failure regardless of actual PTP sync state.


Overall Issue Class: test_regression

(The dominant failure mode — 11/13 — is caused by main test code depending on a daemon log line that exists only in main, not in the release-4.20 images the job is actually testing. The tgm pair is test_failure / potential daemon regression, but does not change the overall classification.)


Overall Severity: HIGH

(11/13 failures are systemic and reproducible across every topology suite in every run that pairs main test scripts with release-4.20 daemon images; the job cannot pass in this configuration without the backport or a test-side workaround.)




Per-Suite Details



bc



Suite: bc (3 failures)


Suite TL;DR: All 3 BC ClockSync tests time out because GetProfileLogID cannot find the Ptp4lConf: #profile: log pattern in the release-4.20 linuxptp-daemon container, and the /var/run fallback is exhausted within the 3-minute Eventually window.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: GetProfileLogID log pattern absent from release-4.20 linuxptp-daemon (affects: all 3 tests — "Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master", "Can provide a profile with higher priority")
• Evidence:

```

could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-g59s2 container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded

```

```

could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-7khz9 container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded

```

```

could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-g59s2 container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=timedout waiting for matches

```

The same error occurs across all four suites (bc, dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha) on different pods (-g59s2, -7khz9, -68lc9, -d75dq), confirming a daemon-wide, systematic failure.


• Root Cause: GetClockIDForeign (called from BasicClockSyncCheck in test/pkg/ptptesthelper/ptptesthelper.go:66) first calls GetProfileLogID (test/pkg/ptphelper/ptphelper.go:84), which searches for the regex (?m).?Ptp4lConf: #profile: (.|\n)?message_tag \[(.)\] in the linuxptp-daemon-container logs. This pattern is only emitted when the linuxptp-daemon calls printNodeProfile(nodeProfile) after configInput = configOutput (linuxptp-daemon/pkg/daemon/daemon.go:1152-1154) writes the rendered config (containing #profile: ) back into nodeProfile.Ptp4lConf. Both the *configInput = configOutput assignment and the unconditional printNodeProfile call at line 1412 were introduced in linuxptp-daemon main PR #617 (commit fd6ff1e, merged June 30 2026), but have not been backported to release-4.20. The CI job runs test scripts from ptp-operator main against release-4.20 production images, so the daemon never emits the expected log line. The /var/run-based fallback (GetConfigForProfileFromVarRun, added in ptp-operator main commit 84723c8 June 22) is invoked only after GetProfileLogID's own 3-minute deadline elapses (~t=200s), which coincides with the outer Eventually timeout (3 min), leaving insufficient time to complete exec commands inside the pod and verify the result. GetClockIDForeign then returns lastErr from the log scan, and Eventually reports failure at ~202s.

• Breaking PR/Commit: linuxptp-daemon main PR #617 (fd6ff1e, June 30 2026) added *configInput = configOutput and the unconditional printNodeProfile that the test requires — but this has not been cherry-picked to release-4.20. Per branch-awareness policy, a main-only change cannot be the root cause; the true underlying issue is that the main test code (GetProfileLogID, introduced in ptp-operator commit 73a75ec, March 25 2026) was written assuming a log-output behavior that exists only on main linuxptp-daemon, not in release-4.20 images. Secondary contributing commit: ptp-operator bb43e35 (June 23 2026) has prerequisite k8snetworkplumbingwg/linuxptp-daemon#215 which also affects daemon startup behavior



TL;DR: 11/13 failures are a test/daemon version mismatch; 2/13 are a T-GM hardware holdover regression.



Root Cause Groups:


• 11/13 failures — Ptp4lConf: #profile: log line absent from release-4.20 daemon (bc: 3, dualfollower: 3, dualnicbc: 3, dualnicbcha: 2)

The test helper GetProfileLogID (test code running from main) searches for a log regex (Ptp4lConf: #profile: ) that is only emitted after *configInput = configOutput + printNodeProfile — code introduced in linuxptp-daemon main PR #617 (commit fd6ff1e, merged 2026-06-30) and not backported to release-4.20. The CI job deploys release-4.20 daemon images (USE_UPSTREAM=false), so the pattern is never logged. GetProfileLogID's internal 3-minute deadline consumes the entire outer Eventually window, leaving the /var/run fallback no time to run. All failures surface as either timedout waiting for matches (stream alive, pattern never appeared) or context deadline exceeded (Kubernetes log-stream dropped), with a secondary cluster-API Unauthorized state observed post-run in dualnicbc/dualnicbcha, consistent with infrastructure stress from 11 concurrent 200-second stream-read timeouts.


• 2/13 failures — T-GM DPLL does not enter holdover on GNSS disruption (tgm: 2)

When GNSS signal is lost or rebooted on WPC T-GM hardware, the Intel DPLL is expected to transition to lockStatus: holdover (clock class 7). Instead, the DPLL stays in or falls directly to FREERUN (clock class 248), so openshift_ptp_clock_class never reaches 7. Both tgm tests fail: one by timeout polling for clock class 7, one by direct metric assertion finding 248. This is independent of the log-line issue; the release-4.20 daemon or underlying firmware may have a regression in holdover state management.



Cross-Suite Patterns:


Root CauseSuites AffectedFailures
#profile: log line missing from release-4.20 daemonbc, dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha11
T-GM DPLL does not enter holdover (clock class 7)tgm2

The 11-failure group is fully cross-suite: all four PTP topology suites hit identical pod-log-stream errors on six different pods across different nodes, ruling out any single pod, node, or suite-specific cause. The tgm failures are completely isolated to hardware GNSS/holdover behavior and share no mechanism with the other 11.



Actionable Recommendations:


1. Backport linuxptp-daemon main PR #617 to release-4.20 (fixes 11/13 failures). The commit fd6ff1e adds *configInput = configOutput and the unconditional printNodeProfile call that emits Ptp4lConf: #profile: into daemon logs. Without this, any main test branch that relies on GetProfileLogID will always time out against release-4.20 images. Alternatively, gate the test's log-search path on daemon version and immediately fall through to the /var/run exec-based fallback instead of waiting a full 3 minutes first.

2. Investigate T-GM DPLL holdover regression on release-4.20 (fixes 2/13 failures). Audit recent commits to linuxptp-daemon/release-4.20 and cloud-event-proxy/release-4.20 for changes in pkg/event/event.go DPLL state-transition logic. Also verify WPC hardware firmware version matches the expected holdover capability; if firmware is the variable, pin firmware version in the CI telco5g cluster.

3. Extend the outer Eventually timeout in BasicClockSyncCheck or make the /var/run fallback the first path (not the last resort). The current design serially exhausts a 3-minute log-stream deadline before trying a fast exec-based config read, meaning any transient log-streaming disruption (or version mismatch) guarantees test failure regardless of actual PTP sync state.


Overall Issue Class: test_regression

(The dominant failure mode — 11/13 — is caused by main test code depending on a daemon log line that exists only in main, not in the release-4.20 images the job is actually testing. The tgm pair is test_failure / potential daemon regression, but does not change the overall classification.)


Overall Severity: HIGH

(11/13 failures are systemic and reproducible across every topology suite in every run that pairs main test scripts with release-4.20 daemon images; the job cannot pass in this configuration without the backport or a test-side workaround.)
